GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "This is the secret to a cozy home according to an interiors author", "There's nothing quite like the feeling of coming home to the perfect space. As our sanctuary from the outside world and the place we can really, truly relax in, our homes play much bigger parts in our lives than just a place to sleep. It's a truth never more evident than right now, with most Australians self-isolating while our cities shut down in an attempt to flatten the coronavirus curve. For author and influencer, Geneva Vanderzeil, it's all about making the most of this time indoors, using the extra days at home to relax, unwind and take each hour as it comes. "For me, I'm trying to see the opportunity in having more time on my hands than I ever have before. I'm also focusing on things that will distract us from the news and reduce the anxiety that comes with being indoors for too long. That means lots of making, doing some artwork, reading, napping and listening to interesting podcasts," she tells Vogue Living. When it comes to crafting the perfect space for her small family, Vanderzeil is all about injecting personality into every corner and ensuring each room puts comfort first. For her, that means embracing some of Koala's best new pieces (as they really understand comfort), including a sofa , bedframe and of course, mattress and sheets. "I like to pick a few vintage items, and then some great new pieces like a comfy sofa, and then I layer in textiles and decor items. It's important to pick timeless furniture that lasts so you can get creative when it comes to decorating and styling. The Koala sofa has a beautiful design–it's hard to pick between the brushtail grey and bonnie doon blue version" she says, noting two of her favourite accessories in the living room as an ottoman and "lots" of cushions. Vanderzeil has been a fan of Koala for some time now, looking to the Australian-made brand to outfit some of her most important rooms: the bedroom and the living room. "Koala's furniture is so comfortable and practical, along with being perfectly minimalist, and their pieces are the ideal foundation for a beautiful and cosy room. Plus, you adopt a turtle with purchase–so you're actually contributing to the environment and saving a species from extinction!" she adds. However, Vanderzeil's top tip for styling a home all comes back to colour. Urging us to consider our colour palette before all else, the author and mother knows the difference a great lick of paint and perfectly paired neutral furniture can make. "I think often when you see a space that doesn't work it can be unclear why, but chances are it's because there isn't any cohesion amongst the colours that are used. I first think about the colours that are in the space already–including the walls and floors – and then I think about some additional base colours and finally pick some highlight colours." She notes how important it is to carefully plan any big purchases and to ensure that these pieces will go the distance–considering a change in style or taste over the years–and on that note, recommends going for neutral sofas and bed frames. "At the moment I'm focused on creating a calming feel in our home, particularly as we spend more time there, and so I've been embracing a minimalist palette of neutrals, lots of soft fluffy texture and some pops of terracotta," Vanderzeil reveals. As for the most important pieces in the house? Vanderzeil is all about crafting her own unique vision within her four walls, but admits there are two pieces that she'll always turn to the experts for. "I'm such a big fan of using DIY to bring personality and liveability into a space, but equally I know that it's worth investing in a really great bed and a beautiful sofa that will stand the test of time and get better with age." Visit: Koala.com.au"
